design_philosophy.core_loop: Tournament-based one-on-one fighting with character progression marked by colored belt unlocks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning opponent patterns and executing timing-based attacks within spherical character constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming a champion fighter while experiencing absurdist humor through unconventional combatant designs (farting monkey, clown, dinosaur, genie)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Core visual innovation: all characters composed entirely of spheres for processor efficiency and consistent perspective-independent rendering. Deliberately eccentric fighter roster prioritizes humor and visual distinction over martial authenticity. Design choice sacrifices traditional fighting game silhouettes for memorable, readable sphere-based forms.

difficulty.checkpointing: Boss defeat progression unlocks colored belts; tournament structure with sequential boss encounters
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three difficulty levels available for player selection

progression.upgrade_system: Colored belt system earned through boss defeats (Red Belt, Green Belt, Blue Belt, Black Belt) changes fighter's color scheme
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeating The Jester (final boss) grants ability to play as bosses in subsequent playthroughs
replayability_hooks: Unlockable boss characters (Guggler, Bounder, T-Wrecks, Lamprey, The Jester)
Lamprey's shapeshifting transformations (El Ballz, Spike, Byte Viper) provide varied boss encounter patterns
Regional character name variations suggest localized experience differences

world_building_techniques: Opening cinematic with Jester introducing the tournament challenge
Character personality expressed through fighting animations (taunts, poses, behavioral quirks)
Absurdist character archetypes (farting monkey, clown, sumo wrestler, ballerina, superhero) establish eccentric universe
Boss roster progression establishes escalating challenge narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Each boss represents distinct archetype with signature movement and attack patterns. Progression escalates through animal/creature variety and supernatural elements (genie final boss). Difficulty structured through pattern complexity and behavioral adaptation.
notable_bosses: Guggler: Ostrich with pecking and beak-tossing attacks; jump-kick combination
Bounder: Kangaroo utilizing boxing gloves, tail-balance stance for leg flinging attacks
T-Wrecks: Tyrannosaurus with aggressive jaw-grab mechanics and devastating tail shockwave attacks
Lamprey: Mystical genie with swift magical attacks and shapeshifting transformations into El Ballz (bull), Spike (scorpion), Byte Viper (snake)
The Jester: Ultimate boss with disassembly/reassembly mechanics, exceptional damage output, multi-phase design (first defeat triggers reassembly for continued combat)

faction_indicators: Tournament structure implies martial competition hierarchy
Belt color system creates visual status markers for player advancement
Boss character roster establishes champion tier distinct from regular fighters
danger_signals: T-Wrecks as aggressive destructive force archetype
The Jester as mysterious controlling figure orchestrating tournament
Lamprey's magical transformation abilities suggesting unpredictable threat level
environmental_storytelling: Arena setting implies tournament venue with spectator context
Character diversity (circus clown, sumo wrestler, ballerina, superhero, caveman) suggests global or multidimensional recruitment

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Ballz (SNES) article
